_readme: This file is the single source of truth for both portfolio pages. Edit it at /edit/ in your browser, or edit it here directly. Text fields support **double asterisks** to highlight key terms.

profile.name: Md Abu Sayeb Rayhan
profile.roleResearch: Computer Vision · Embodied AI · Multimodal Vision-Language Models
profile.availability: Seeking research-based Master's positions and AI engineering roles
profile.summaryIndustry: I build computer-vision and language systems that run in production, not just in notebooks. Over eighteen months at **Othello Financial Services** in Osaka I took real-time video analytics from prototype to deployment on Azure, cutting inference latency by **70%** and production costs by **50%**. Before that I led a three-person team delivering a **multilingual OCR and retrieval system** for a Bangladesh government project, end to end. The problems I enjoy are the ones where a model has to hold up against messy data, a latency budget and actual users.
profile.summaryResearch: I am a computer-vision researcher with three peer-reviewed papers and eighteen months of applied experience deploying perception systems at industrial scale. My work sits where **visual perception**, **generative modelling** and **language understanding** meet, and I am seeking a research-based Master's position to pursue that intersection more rigorously.

news.text: Submitted **RGEF: Reliability-Gated Evidential Fusion for Robust Medical AI** to the IEEE International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (**RAAICON 2026**).
Seeking **research-based Master's positions** for the 2026–27 intake, alongside AI engineering roles.
Presented two papers at **IEEE QPAIN 2026** and received the **Best Presenter Award**.
Concluded eighteen months as Data Scientist at **Othello Financial Services**, Osaka, and returned to Dhaka.
Joined **Othello Financial Services**, Osaka, as a **Data Scientist** on the AGiFT AI Camera team.
Graduated from **Comilla University** with a BSc in Computer Science & Engineering.
Joined a **Bangladesh government project** as AI Engineer, leading a three-person team until July 2024.
Began work as a **Research Assistant** at Comilla University under Partha Chakraborty, on human–robot interaction and OCR.

metrics.label: Inference latency reduced in a real-time analytics pipeline
Production cost cut through automated video generation
Detection accuracy on custom-trained YOLO models
Peer-reviewed papers published, plus one under review

researchStatement: My research concerns how machines build a usable understanding of the visual world — and what happens to that understanding once it leaves the benchmark. I work on **multi-object tracking**, **human activity recognition** and **pose estimation**, and increasingly on the generative side of the same question: how **diffusion models** can synthesise the data that perception systems are starved of.

That pairing is deliberate rather than incidental. My undergraduate thesis fine-tuned Stable Diffusion for controllable image synthesis, which taught me how much structure a generative model carries about the scenes it produces. I then spent eighteen months building perception pipelines that had to survive contact with deployment — occlusion, motion blur, camera drift and a fixed latency budget. Reading those two experiences against each other is what convinced me the interesting problem is not perception or generation alone, but the loop between them.

I am drawn to **embodied AI** and **multimodal vision-language models** for that reason: they are the settings where perception, generation and action stop being separable. I would like to pursue this in a research-based Master's programme, working on grounded scene understanding and on generative simulation as a route around the data bottleneck that constrains embodied agents.

researchAreas.title: Robot & Scene Perception
researchAreas.desc: Multi-object tracking, pose estimation and activity recognition in cluttered, unscripted environments.

researchAreas.title: Embodied AI
researchAreas.desc: Coupling perception to action, and grounding agent behaviour in continuous sensory streams rather than curated datasets.

researchAreas.title: Multimodal Vision-Language Models
researchAreas.desc: Grounded scene understanding, instruction following and cross-modal reasoning.

researchAreas.title: Generative Simulation
researchAreas.desc: Diffusion models for synthetic data and simulation, aimed at the data bottleneck in embodied learning.

publications.title: RGEF: Reliability-Gated Evidential Fusion for Robust Medical AI
publications.venue: IEEE International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON) 2026
publications.note: Evidential deep learning for medical decision support, using **reliability gating** to weight each modality by how trustworthy its evidence is — so the model abstains rather than guessing when inputs are unreliable.

publications.title: Forensic Invariant Learning for Synthetic Image Detection: Bridging Benford's Law and Topological Analysis with Machine Learning Ensembles
publications.authors: **Md Abu Sayeb Rayhan**, Ajoy Shutradhar, Hustagna Hiya
publications.venue: IEEE International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence and Networking (QPAIN) 2026
publications.note: Detecting AI-generated imagery through **physics-informed invariants** rather than learned artefacts. Combines Benford's Law digit statistics with topological features across a six-model ensemble, targeting cues that survive a change of generator — the failure mode most detectors share.

publications.title: BD Eye-Doc: When Computer Vision Meets Conversational AI for Scalable Eye Disease Screening in Bangladesh
publications.authors: Ajoy Shutradhar, **Md Abu Sayeb Rayhan**, Mahmuda Khatun, Bishwajit Das
publications.venue: IEEE International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence and Networking (QPAIN) 2026
publications.note: Pairs an image-classification pipeline with a conversational interface so that screening can be delivered where ophthalmologists are scarce. The design question is less about accuracy than about **deployability in low-resource clinical settings**.

publications.title: Is CNN Dead? A Comprehensive Empirical Analysis of Convolutional Neural Networks, Ensemble Methods, and Vision Transformers for Rice Leaf Disease Classification in Low-Resource Agricultural Settings
publications.venue: International Conference on Emerging Computing and Communication Technologies (ECCT) 2026 · Advances in Computer Science Research, Atlantis Press (Springer Nature)
publications.note: A controlled comparison of CNNs, ensembles and **Vision Transformers** under the compute and data constraints that actually obtain in agricultural deployment — where the strongest architecture on ImageNet is rarely the right choice.

talks.title: Forensic Invariant Learning for Synthetic Image Detection
BD Eye-Doc: Computer Vision Meets Conversational AI for Eye Disease Screening
Is CNN Dead? CNNs, Ensembles and Vision Transformers for Rice Leaf Disease Classification

bullets: Built **multi-object tracking** (ByteTrack), **pose estimation** (MoveNet) and **human activity recognition** (MMAction2) pipelines for automated behaviour analysis on live retail video.
Designed a spatial clustering pipeline applying **OPTICS** to pose-estimation output for group-dynamics modelling; parallel inference optimisation reduced processing latency by **~70%**.
Developed a video **event-segmentation** system on a fine-tuned YOLO detector at **90%+ accuracy**, automating highlight extraction and cutting production cost by **50%**.
Implemented a **retrieval-augmented generation** system (LangChain, Pinecone, GPT-4) for context-aware document querying, deployed on **Microsoft Azure**.
Integrated IoT edge devices feeding real-time computer vision pipelines for automated data acquisition.
Developed a multilingual (Bangla/English) **NLP and OCR** retrieval pipeline spanning PDF, Word, Excel and image formats, combining optical character recognition with **dense embeddings**.
Shipped as a desktop application built with PyQt and ChromaDB.
**Led a three-person team** across the full product lifecycle, from design through deployment.
Conducted empirical studies in **Human–Robot Interaction** and **Optical Character Recognition**, covering dataset design, experimental protocol and quantitative analysis.
Prepared and validated results subsequently incorporated into **peer-reviewed conference publications**.

projects.title: Universal NFT Generation Platform
projects.desc: Generalised a sports-specific video system into a platform handling user-generated footage across festivals, concerts and other domains. Fine-tuned action detection models and built the pipeline for automated clip creation.

projects.title: Sports Highlight Generation System
projects.desc: Trained a custom object detection model and built a real-time video processing pipeline that identifies key moments in sports footage and generates clips without human intervention.

projects.title: Real-Time In-Store Behaviour Analytics
projects.desc: Real-time system detecting customer groupings and product interest from in-store camera feeds using pose estimation and density-based spatial clustering. Parallelised inference brought latency down sharply.

projects.title: RAG Customer Support Chatbot
projects.desc: End-to-end retrieval-augmented chatbot that retrieves relevant context from a vector index and generates grounded answers. Built and deployed solo on Azure.

projects.title: Context Searcher
projects.desc: RAG-based desktop application for keyword and semantic search across PDF, DOCX, XLSX, TXT and images in Bengali and English, combining OCR with dense embeddings.

projects.title: ADAS Lane & Vehicle Detection
projects.desc: Real-time lane boundary and vehicle detection for driver-assistance systems, combining a YOLOv5 detector fine-tuned on a custom dataset with classical computer vision for lane geometry.

education.coursework: Artificial Intelligence, Algorithm Design, Theory of Computation, Introduction to Data Science, Object-Oriented Programming, Research-Based Project.
education.thesisDesc: Fine-tuned **Stable Diffusion** with a U-Net backbone on a purpose-built dataset of Bangladeshi landmarks, including Salbon Bihar and the Comilla University Gate. Examined prompt engineering and **conditioning mechanisms** as levers on generative fidelity, with OpenCV post-processing for artefact reduction.
